steps.title: Make a perfect square
steps.content:
Move all terms to one side:
$$169x^2+104x-84=0$$
The first two terms match part of $(13x+4)^2$:
$$(13x+4)^2=169x^2+104x+16$$

steps.title: Use the positive value
steps.content:
Rewrite the equation with that square:
$$(13x+4)^2-100=0$$
$$(13x+4)^2=100$$
Because $13x+4\gt0$, use the positive square root. The answer is 10.
